Let's discover Docusaurus in less than 5 minutes.
An online mathematical encyclopedia devoted to Deriving and exploring mathematics beyond the classroom for high school students. All Things Are Derivable

Our mission is to make mathematical reasoning transparent, tangible, and telling through Derivation while offering additional pathways for inquisitive and motivated students to explore mathematics with greter rigor and depth.
This project aims to demystify mathematical Deriving and to foster deeper curiosity through clear and careful reasoning.

Throughout this project, Derivation is treated as a proper noun, referring to the process of obtain a result through meticulous, step-by-step calculation. As the main theme of Art of Derivation, all words related to Derivation (such as Derive, Deriving) are highlighted in the main theme color. Some closely related terms, though not strictly equivalent, including Proof, Justification, and Logical Argument, all of which are integral to the idea of Derivation presented in this project. My understanding of Derivation was mainly developed from my AP Physics C: Mechanics course, where the Free Response Questions frequently require student to Derive expressions from fundamental definitions or equations from the provided equations sheet.
This project is built on Docusaurus and deployed on GitHub Pages. Below are detailed format information.

All documents are stored as Markdown files and use either the .md or .mdx extension. All document file names are capitalized and connected using hyphens (-). All documents are stored under the docs directory, organized by sidebars.js. All referenced images (such as .jpg, .png, .svg) are stored in the static/img directory.
All documents start with the front matter to ensure the consistent format and navigation. More additional details, refer to Markdown Features | Docusaurus.
All mathematical expressions are rendered using KaTeX with remark-math and follow standard LaTeX\LaTeXLATEŌĆŗX syntax.

All Derivation processes are placed in collapsible blocks, implemented using <details>+<summary>. All Derivation processes are organized using the align* enviornment. All Derivation processes are completed with \blacksquare (Ō¢Ā\blacksquareŌ¢Ā), indicating Q.E.D. (quod erat demonstrandum), meaning "that which was to be demonstrated".

Note The Gray Adomination provides extra and unnecessary notes and information. Definition The Green Adomination provides definition about the given topic. Property, Identity, Solution... The Blue Adomination provides Properties, Identities, Solutions, and so on that is NOT designed to show the collapsable Derivation process. Derivation The collapsable Adomination provides detailed process for Derivation Warning The Orange Adomination provides information that is important to know.

The theme color of this project is Indigo (#4b0082) in light mode and Magenta (#ff61ff) in dark mode. The theme color is exclusively reserved for Derivation and its related concepts, rather than decorative styling.

Below are (30/147) advanced integral formulas listed in the appendix of the Higher Mathematics (Tongji University). I will Derive each formula step by step. It may seem futile and impractival to memorize all the formulas, but it is a great opportunity to consolidate integration techniques and appreciate heuristics behind them.

In my AP Physics C Mechanics class, many students were struggling to Derive the terminal velocity of a free-falling object, and the primary reason was that they can't solve the Integral Formula #21. My teacher back then found a really sophisticated way through hyperbolic trigonometry substitution, and everyone was stunned by the complexity, but I came up with a simple algebraic transformation. An example is below: Solution Let: u=x3u=x^3u=x3 dudx=3x2du=3x2 dx\begin{align*} \frac{\mathrm{d}u}{\mathrm{d}x}&=3x^2\\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}&=\color{red}{3x^2\ \mathrm{d}x}\\ \end{align*}dxduŌĆŗduŌĆŗ=3x2=3x2 dxŌĆŗ Plug in back to the question: Ōł½3x2sinŌüĪx3 dx=Ōł½sinŌüĪu du=Ōł½sinŌüĪu du=ŌłÆcosŌüĪu+C=ŌłÆcosŌüĪx3+C\begin{align*} \int{\color{red}{3x^2}}\sin{x^3}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}x}&=\int{\sin{u}}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}\\ &=\int{\sin{u}}\ \mathrm{d}u\\ &=-\cos{u}+C\\ &=-\cos{x^3}+C \end{align*}Ōł½3x2sinx3 dxŌĆŗ=Ōł½sinu du=Ōł½sinu du=ŌłÆcosu+C=ŌłÆcosx3+CŌĆŗ Hence: Ōł½3x2sinŌüĪx3 dx=ŌłÆcosŌüĪx3+CŌ¢Ā\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=-\cos{x^3}+C \tag*{$\blacksquare$}Ōł½3x2sinx3 dx=ŌłÆcosx3+CŌ¢Ā By replacing the variable xxx into a function of uuu, we can simplify the overall question to a common integral. As you can see, this is a method we inversely use the Chain Rule, and generally, it is called: The substitution rule Given a differenciable function u=g(x)u=g(x)u=g(x), and fff is continuous on its interval, then: Ōł½f(g(x))gŌĆ▓(x) dx=Ōł½f(u) du\int{f\left(g\left(x\right)\right)g'(x)}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f\left(u\right)}\ \mathrm{d}uŌł½f(g(x))gŌĆ▓(x) dx=Ōł½f(u) du Definite Integral In the case of Definite Integral, the bound should also changes, as: Ōł½abf(g(x))gŌĆ▓(x) dx=Ōł½g(a)g(b)f(u) du\int_{a}^{b}{f\left(g\left(x\right)\right)g'(x)}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{g(a)}^{g(b)}{f\left(u\right)}\ \mathrm{d}uŌł½abŌĆŗf(g(x))gŌĆ▓(x) dx=Ōł½g(a)g(b)ŌĆŗf(u) du

Direct Substitution is a method created by the author himself. It simplify the process of substitution without write out uuu and du\mathrm{d}udu. I will show this with an example: Ōł½3x2sinŌüĪx3 dx= ?\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\ ?Ōł½3x2sinx3 dx= ? This time, let's try what I called Direct Substituton: Direct Substitution Approach After analyze, we want to substitute x3x^3x3. All we need to do is substitude dx\mathrm{d}xdx to a fraction where the top is dx3\mathrm{d}x^3dx3 and the bottom is the derivative of x3x^3x3 based on xxx, as: Ōł½3x2sinŌüĪx3 dx=Ōł½3x2sinŌüĪx3 d(x3)3x2=Ōł½sinŌüĪx3 d(x3)\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{\cancel{3x^2}\sin{x^3}}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(x^3)}{\cancel{3x^2}}=\int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)Ōł½3x2sinx3 dx=Ōł½3x2sinx3 3x2d(x3)ŌĆŗ=Ōł½sinx3 d(x3) And all we need is to consider x3x^3x3 as uuu and integrate normally: Ō¤╣ Ōł½sinŌüĪx3 d(x3)=ŌłÆcosŌüĪx3+C\implies \int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}+CŌ¤╣Ōł½sinx3 d(x3)=ŌłÆcosx3+C More importantly, for definite integral, it doesn't need to change bounds, as: Ōł½ab3x2sinŌüĪx3 dx=Ōł½absinŌüĪx3 d(x3)=ŌłÆcosŌüĪx3ŌłŻab=ŌłÆcosŌüĪb3+cosŌüĪa3\int_{a}^{b}3x^2\sin{x^3}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{a}^{b}{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}\big|_{a}^{b}=-\cos{b^3}+\cos{a^3}Ōł½abŌĆŗ3x2sinx3 dx=Ōł½abŌĆŗsinx3 d(x3)=ŌłÆcosx3ŌĆŗabŌĆŗ=ŌłÆcosb3+cosa3 Direct Substitution In general, if we want to substitude ax+bax+bax+b or term that can cancel the other one, we may use direct substitution Ōł½f(x)g(x)ŌÅ¤Substitude dx=Ōł½f(x)g(x) d(g(x))gŌĆ▓(x)\int{{f(x)}\underbrace{g(x)}_{\text{Substitude}}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f(x)g(x)}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(g(x))}{g'(x)} Ōł½f(x)Substitudeg(x)ŌĆŗŌĆŗ dx=Ōł½f(x)g(x) gŌĆ▓(x)d(g(x))ŌĆŗ

sinŌüĪ(╬▒+╬▓)=sinŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓+cosŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓\sin{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\sin{\alpha}\cos{\beta}+\cos{\alpha}\sin{\beta}sin(╬▒+╬▓)=sin╬▒cos╬▓+cos╬▒sin╬▓ Derivation sinŌüĪ╬▓=BCAB=BC1 Ō¤╣ BC=sinŌüĪ╬▓cosŌüĪ╬▓=ACAB=AC1 Ō¤╣ AC=cosŌüĪ╬▓sinŌüĪ╬▒=CDAC=CDcosŌüĪ╬▓ Ō¤╣ CD=sinŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓cosŌüĪ╬▒=CFBC=CFsinŌüĪ╬▓ Ō¤╣ CF=cosŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AC}=\frac{CD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies CD&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BC}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sin╬▓=ABBCŌĆŗ=1BCŌĆŗŌ¤╣BCcos╬▓=ABACŌĆŗ=1ACŌĆŗŌ¤╣ACsin╬▒=ACCDŌĆŗ=cos╬▓CDŌĆŗŌ¤╣CDcos╬▒=BCCFŌĆŗ=sin╬▓CFŌĆŗŌ¤╣CFŌĆŗ=sin╬▓=cos╬▓=sin╬▒cos╬▓=cos╬▒sin╬▓ŌĆŗsinŌüĪ(╬▒+╬▓)=BEAB=BE1=DF=CD+CF=sinŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓+cosŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓ \begin{align*} \sin{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BE}{AB}=\frac{BE}{1}=DF\\ &=CD+CF\\ &=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}+\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin(╬▒+╬▓)ŌĆŗ=ABBEŌĆŗ=1BEŌĆŗ=DF=CD+CF=sin╬▒cos╬▓+cos╬▒sin╬▓ŌĆŗŌ¢ĀŌĆŗ

sinŌüĪ(╬▒ŌłÆ╬▓)=sinŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓ŌłÆcosŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓\sin{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\sin{\alpha}\cos{\beta}-\cos{\alpha}\sin{\beta}sin(╬▒ŌłÆ╬▓)=sin╬▒cos╬▓ŌłÆcos╬▒sin╬▓ Derivation sinŌüĪ╬▓=BCAB=BC1 Ō¤╣ BC=sinŌüĪ╬▓cosŌüĪ╬▓=ACAB=AC1 Ō¤╣ AC=cosŌüĪ╬▓sinŌüĪ╬▒=ADAC=ADcosŌüĪ╬▓ Ō¤╣ AD=sinŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓cosŌüĪ╬▒=BFBC=BFsinŌüĪ╬▓ Ō¤╣ BF=cosŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{AC}=\frac{AD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies AD&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{BC}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sin╬▓=ABBCŌĆŗ=1BCŌĆŗŌ¤╣BCcos╬▓=ABACŌĆŗ=1ACŌĆŗŌ¤╣ACsin╬▒=ACADŌĆŗ=cos╬▓ADŌĆŗŌ¤╣ADcos╬▒=BCBFŌĆŗ=sin╬▓BFŌĆŗŌ¤╣BFŌĆŗ=sin╬▓=cos╬▓=sin╬▒cos╬▓=cos╬▒sin╬▓ŌĆŗsinŌüĪ(╬▒ŌłÆ╬▓)=BGAB=BG1=AE=ADŌłÆED=ADŌłÆBF=sinŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓ŌłÆcosŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓ \begin{align*} \sin{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BG}{AB}=\frac{BG}{1}=AE\\ &=AD-ED\\ &=AD-BF\\ &=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}-\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin(╬▒ŌłÆ╬▓)ŌĆŗ=ABBGŌĆŗ=1BGŌĆŗ=AE=ADŌłÆED=ADŌłÆBF=sin╬▒cos╬▓ŌłÆcos╬▒sin╬▓ŌĆŗŌ¢ĀŌĆŗ

cosŌüĪ(╬▒ŌłÆ╬▓)=cosŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓+sinŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓\cos{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\cos{\alpha}\cos{\beta}+\sin{\alpha}\sin{\beta}cos(╬▒ŌłÆ╬▓)=cos╬▒cos╬▓+sin╬▒sin╬▓ Derivation sinŌüĪ╬▓=BCAB=BC1 Ō¤╣ BC=sinŌüĪ╬▓cosŌüĪ╬▓=ACAB=AC1 Ō¤╣ AC=cosŌüĪ╬▓sinŌüĪ╬▒=CFBC=CFsinŌüĪ╬▓ Ō¤╣ CF=sinŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓cosŌüĪ╬▒=CDAC=CDcosŌüĪ╬▓ Ō¤╣ CD=cosŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BC}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AC}=\frac{CD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies CD&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sin╬▓=ABBCŌĆŗ=1BCŌĆŗŌ¤╣BCcos╬▓=ABACŌĆŗ=1ACŌĆŗŌ¤╣ACsin╬▒=BCCFŌĆŗ=sin╬▓CFŌĆŗŌ¤╣CFcos╬▒=ACCDŌĆŗ=cos╬▓CDŌĆŗŌ¤╣CDŌĆŗ=sin╬▓=cos╬▓=sin╬▒sin╬▓=cos╬▒cos╬▓ŌĆŗcosŌüĪ(╬▒ŌłÆ╬▓)=AGAB=AG1=DF=CD+CF=cosŌüĪ╬▒cosŌüĪ╬▓+sinŌüĪ╬▒sinŌüĪ╬▓ \begin{align*} \cos{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{AG}{AB}=\frac{AG}{1}=DF\\ &=CD+CF\\ &=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}+\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos(╬▒ŌłÆ╬▓)ŌĆŗ=ABAGŌĆŗ=1AGŌĆŗ=DF=CD+CF=cos╬▒cos╬▓+sin╬▒sin╬▓ŌĆŗŌ¢ĀŌĆŗ

tanŌüĪ(╬▒+╬▓)=tanŌüĪ╬▒+tanŌüĪ╬▓1ŌłÆtanŌüĪ╬▒tanŌüĪ╬▓\tan{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\frac{\tan{\alpha}+\tan{\beta}}{1-\tan{\alpha}\tan{\beta}}tan(╬▒+╬▓)=1ŌłÆtan╬▒tan╬▓tan╬▒+tan╬▓ŌĆŗ Derivation tanŌüĪ╬▒=CDAD=CD1 Ō¤╣ CD=tanŌüĪ╬▒secŌüĪ╬▒=ACAD=AC1 Ō¤╣ AC=secŌüĪ╬▒tanŌüĪ╬▓=BCAC=BCsecŌüĪ╬▒ Ō¤╣ BC=secŌüĪ╬▒tanŌüĪ╬▓secŌüĪ╬▒=BCCF=secŌüĪ╬▒tanŌüĪ╬▓CF Ō¤╣ CF=tanŌüĪ╬▓tanŌüĪ╬▒=BFCF=BFtanŌüĪ╬▓ Ō¤╣ BF=tanŌüĪ╬▒tanŌüĪ╬▓ \begin{align*} \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AD}=\frac{CD}{1}\implies CD&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{AC}{AD}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\\ \blue{\tan{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AC}=\frac{BC}{\green{\sec{\alpha}}}\implies BC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{BC}{CF}=\frac{\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{CF}\implies CF&=\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{CF}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\tan{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}} \end{align*}tan╬▒=ADCDŌĆŗ=1CDŌĆŗŌ¤╣CDsec╬▒=ADACŌĆŗ=1ACŌĆŗŌ¤╣ACtan╬▓=ACBCŌĆŗ=sec╬▒BCŌĆŗŌ¤╣BCsec╬▒=CFBCŌĆŗ=CFsec╬▒tan╬▓ŌĆŗŌ¤╣CFtan╬▒=CFBFŌĆŗ=tan╬▓BFŌĆŗŌ¤╣BFŌĆŗ=tan╬▒=sec╬▒=sec╬▒tan╬▓=tan╬▓=tan╬▒tan╬▓ŌĆŗtanŌüĪ(╬▒+╬▓)=BEAE=DFAE=CD+CFADŌłÆBF=tanŌüĪ╬▒+tanŌüĪ╬▓1ŌłÆtanŌüĪ╬▒tanŌüĪ╬▓ \begin{align*} \tan{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BE}{AE}=\frac{DF}{AE}=\frac{CD+CF}{AD-BF}\\ &=\frac{\green{\tan{\alpha}}+\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{1-\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}tan(╬▒+╬▓)ŌĆŗ=AEBEŌĆŗ=AEDFŌĆŗ=ADŌłÆBFCD+CFŌĆŗ=1ŌłÆtan╬▒tan╬▓tan╬▒+tan╬▓ŌĆŗŌĆŗŌ¢ĀŌĆŗ

Let's discover Docusaurus in less than 5 minutes.
Get started by creating a new site. Or try Docusaurus immediately with docusaurus.new.
Node.js version 20.0 or above: When installing Node.js, you are recommended to check all checkboxes related to dependencies.

Generate a new Docusaurus site using the classic template. The classic template will automatically be added to your project after you run the command: npm init docusaurus@latest my-website classic You can type this command into Command Prompt, Powershell, Terminal, or any other integrated terminal of your code editor. The command also installs all necessary dependencies you need to run Docusaurus.

Run the development server: cd my-website npm run start The cd command changes the directory you're working with. In order to work with your newly created Docusaurus site, you'll need to navigate the terminal there. The npm run start command builds your website locally and serves it through a development server, ready for you to view at http://localhost:3000/. Open docs/intro.md (this page) and edit some lines: the site reloads automatically and displays your changes.
